title: TC001-User Registration Success
description: Verify that a user can successfully register a new account with valid email and password within 2 minutes.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None
    
    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()
        
        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )
        
        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)
        
        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass
        
        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass
        
        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # Navigate to the registration page by clicking the 'Get Started' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div[2]/a[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Click the 'Sign up' button to navigate to the registration page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Fill in the email, password, and confirm password fields with valid data and submit the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testwriter@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestPass123!')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[4]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestPass123!')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Clear the email field and input a new unique email, then submit the registration form again.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testwriter+1@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Since email verification code is not available, click the 'verify via email link' to simulate verification and proceed to welcome flow.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div[3]/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Try to resend the verification code by clicking the 'Resend' button to simulate receiving a new code or trigger verification process.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Assert that the user is redirected to the 'Verify Your Email' page after registration
        assert await frame.locator('text=Verify Your Email').is_visible()
        # Assert that the instructions to enter the 6-digit verification code are visible
        assert await frame.locator('text=Enter the 6-digit verification code sent to your email address').is_visible()
        # Assert that the verification code input field is present
        assert await frame.locator('input[placeholder="Verification Code"]').is_visible()
        # Assert that the 'Verify Email' button is visible
        assert await frame.locator('text=Verify Email').is_visible()
        # Assert that the 'Didn't receive the code? Resend' option is visible
        assert await frame.locator('text=Didn\'t receive the code? Resend').is_visible()
        # Assert that the 'Back to Sign In' option is visible
        assert await frame.locator('text=Back to Sign In').is_visible()
        # Additional assertion to check that the registration process completed within 2 minutes (120000 ms)
        # This assumes a timer or timestamp is available; if not, this is a placeholder for timing logic
        # Example: assert registration_time <= 120000  # registration_time should be measured during test execution
        # Since user data storage and encryption cannot be verified via UI, assume backend verification or logs are checked separately
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
            
asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-User Registration Failure on Invalid Input
description: Verify registration fails and appropriate error messages are shown when invalid email or weak password is submitted.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None
    
    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()
        
        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )
        
        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)
        
        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass
        
        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass
        
        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # Navigate to the registration page by clicking the 'Get Started' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div[2]/a[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Click the 'Sign up' button to navigate to the registration page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Enter invalid email and weak password, then submit the registration form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('invalid-email')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('123')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[4]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('123')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Test weak password validation by entering a valid email and a weak password, then submit the form to verify the appropriate error message.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('valid.email@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('123')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[4]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('123')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Assert that the invalid email error message is displayed after submitting the form with invalid email
        invalid_email_error = await frame.locator('text=Please enter a valid email address').is_visible()
        assert invalid_email_error, 'Expected error message for invalid email is not displayed'
          
        # Assert that the weak password error message is displayed after submitting the form with weak password
        weak_password_error = await frame.locator('text=Password must be at least 6 characters').is_visible()
        assert weak_password_error, 'Expected error message for weak password is not displayed'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
            
asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-User Login Success with Valid Credentials
description: Verify that a registered user can log in successfully using valid email and password, receiving a valid JWT token.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None
    
    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()
        
        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )
        
        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)
        
        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass
        
        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass
        
        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # Click the Sign In button to navigate to the login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Enter registered email and password into the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test Writer')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestPass123!')
        

        # Click the Sign in button to submit the login form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Clear the email input field, enter a valid registered email with '@' symbol, then click Sign in button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('test.writer@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Verify credentials correctness or try alternative valid credentials if available, or check for additional login error details.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        # Verify if the registered email and password are correct or try to retrieve/reset password or check for other login options.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Enter registered email address to send password reset link.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('test.writer@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Click 'Remember your password? Sign in' button to return to login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Enter registered email and password, then click Sign in to attempt login again.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('test.writer@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestPass123!')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Extract any available error messages or token information from the page or browser storage to verify JWT token issuance or diagnose failure.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Since the task is to verify login with registered user, click 'Sign in' button to return to login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        assert False, 'Test plan execution failed: generic failure assertion.'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
            
asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
The registered user was unable to log in successfully using the provided valid email and password. Multiple login attempts resulted in 'Login failed' messages, and no JWT token was issued or stored for session management. Password reset was initiated but did not resolve the login failure. Therefore, the task to verify successful login and JWT token issuance is not successful with the current credentials and system state.

title: TC004-User Login Failure with Invalid Credentials
description: Verify login fails with incorrect password or non-existent email, showing an error message without exposing sensitive information.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None
    
    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well

                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # Input valid email and incorrect password

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testwriter@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        error_message = frame.locator('text=Login failed')
        assert await error_message.is_visible(), 'Expected error message "Login failed" to be visible after failed login attempt'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
            
asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC005-Password Reset Workflow Success
description: Verify the password reset process completes successfully: user requests reset, receives email, and sets new password.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None
    
    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()
        
        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )
        
        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)
        
        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass
        
        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass
        
        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # Click on 'Sign In' button to go to login page where password reset option is likely available.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Click on 'Forgot your password?' button to navigate to password reset request page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Enter registered email 'Test Writer' and click 'Send reset link' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test Writer')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Clear the invalid email input and enter a valid registered email address with proper format, then submit the reset request again.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testwriter@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Simulate opening the reset link from the email and navigate to the password reset form to enter a new password.
        await page.goto('http://localhost:3000/reset-password-link', timeout=10000)
        

        # Return to login page or homepage to verify if there is an alternative way to complete password reset or to report issue.
        await page.goto('http://localhost:3000/login', timeout=10000)
        

        # Navigate back to the login page and verify if there is any alternative way to complete the password reset or report the issue.
        await page.goto('http://localhost:3000/login', timeout=10000)
        

        # Navigate back to the homepage to check for any alternative password reset options or to report the issue.
        await page.goto('http://localhost:3000', timeout=10000)
        

        # Click on 'Sign In' button to attempt login with old password to verify if password remains unchanged.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Enter registered email and old password, then click 'Sign in' to verify login with old password.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testwriter@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestPass123!')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        assert False, 'Password reset process failed: expected result unknown, forcing failure.'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
            
asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Project Creation and Basic Metadata Setup
description: Verify that a user can create a new writing project with required metadata fields and the project appears in the project dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None
    
    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()
        
        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )
        
        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)
        
        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass
        
        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass
        
        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # Click on the 'Sign In' button to start login process.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/header/div/div/div[2]/a/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Input email and password, then click 'Sign in' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test Writer')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('TestPass123!')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Clear the email field, input a valid email format for the user, then retry login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('testwriter@example.com')
        

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        # Retry login by clicking 'Sign in' button again or check for error messages.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=html/body/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        

        assert False, 'Test plan execution failed: Unable to verify project creation and listing in dashboard.'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
    
    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()
            
asyncio.run(run_test())
